mentions.note: Only cruise line liked; higher-margin ship economics, insulated from travel disruption in key geographies. Carnival/Royal Caribbean too risky on oil/disruption.
Down 2.7%; upscale cruise operator weakening; high-end consumer also pulling back.

Luxury cruise operator with 5-year Condé Nast leadership; contrarian expansion into Egypt post-2011 revolution and ocean cruises post-2012 crisis; 7-continent reach, strong pricing power from affluent guests.
Cramer explicitly recommends buying as cruise line worth holding 'through thick and thin'—company to own long-term, not day-trade; better choice than Norwegian Cruise for investors with discipline.
Kramer's choice over Carnival in cruise line space for next 5 years; stronger fundamentals and operational execution.
Upscale cruise model (no kids, no gambling); all cruise lines well-run but Viking has best pricing power. Benefits from lower fuel costs and reopened destinations post-war.
